content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Static review found four correctness gaps in the new success reconciliation flow: the normal happy path cannot recover run metadata after completion, the multi-repo path reads the wrong workspace, crash replay can skip the required br sync, and the durable success record still omits task_id.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1441-1442 invokes success reconciliation only after the task has fully completed, but src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1504-1532 then requires run_snapshot.active_run or interrupted_run to recover run_id/started_at. Successful runs clear both cursors before control returns to the daemon (src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5530-5535), so the normal happy path falls into the None branch and returns before any bead close, sync, or milestone update happens.
   Rationale: This makes the single-repo happy path a no-op for exactly the success case this change is supposed to handle.
2. In multi-repo mode, process_task_multi_repo passes store_dir into try_reconcile_success (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:603-606, 911-913), but the task's Ralph project is created/read under repo_root (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:709-710). try_reconcile_success uses its base_dir argument for read_project_record/read_run_snapshot (src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1485-1507), so reconciliation looks in the daemon data directory instead of the checked-out repo and returns early without touching the bead.
   Rationale: Even if the run-cursor issue above is fixed, the multi-repo path still points reconciliation at the wrong workspace, so bead closure and milestone updates cannot work there.
3. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:141-144 only runs br sync --flush-only when close_bead_idempotent reports a fresh close. That breaks the required crash-safe idempotency: if the process dies after br close mutates local bead state but before sync, a rerun will observe the bead as already closed and permanently skip the flush. A fresh BrMutationAdapter starts with no pending-mutation knowledge (src/adapters/br_process.rs:449-463), so the already-closed path still needs to issue the post-close sync.
   Rationale: A successful close that was never flushed leaves the bead mutation unpersisted and violates both the ordering and idempotency requirements.
4. update_milestone_state receives task_id but drops it when persisting the success outcome: it only calls record_bead_completion with bead/project/run metadata (src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:238-271). The durable lineage row and completion journal payload have no task_id field (src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:354-376, 475-500), so this change still does not record the required task_id + bead_id + outcome + timestamp linkage outcome.
   Rationale: The implementation updates existing milestone/run lineage, but it never creates a durable task-to-bead success record, so one of the explicit acceptance requirements is still unmet.
